title: Save $500 on this Acer 16 Predator Helios Neo 16 IPS Gaming laptop just $1,499.99 gets you a powerful portable gaming rig with an RTX 5070, 32GB DDR5, and Intel Core Ultra 9 275 HX processor
summary: Acer's Predator Helios Neo 16 IPS Gaming laptop is now available for $1,499.99, offering a powerful portable gaming rig with an RTX 5070, 32GB DDR5, and Intel Core Ultra 9 275 HX processor, representing a $500 discount from its original price.
insight: High-performance gaming at an affordable price point

title: ViewSonic VX2738-2K 27-inch OLED review: An OLED value play
summary: The ViewSonic VX2738-2K 27-inch OLED monitor provides high-performance gaming at a low price. It boasts 240 Hz, Adaptive-Sync, HDR, and Quantum Dot color for smooth, quick, and saturated visuals. This monitor is a great value for gamers seeking high-end features.
insight: Affordable high-performance OLED gaming monitor with premium features

title: Nvidia reveals AI robots that taught themselves to install GPUs into motherboards video shows robot solve high-precision tasks like installing GPUs all by itself
summary: Nvidia showcases robots that can teach themselves high-precision tasks like installing GPUs. These agentic robots can perform real-world tasks with dexterity and precision. The technology has potential applications in various industries.
insight: Autonomous robots can automate complex, high-precision tasks with minimal human oversight.

title: U.S. gov't asks court to dismiss NAACP lawsuit against Elon Musk's xAI over use of unpermitted gas turbines DOJ says Grok model running at Colossus 2 supports mission-critical operations
summary: The US government seeks dismissal of a NAACP lawsuit against xAI over Colossus 2 operations, citing national security concerns.
insight: National security interests take precedence over environmental and social concerns in this case.

title: SMI says Nvidia is driving its consumer PCIe 6.0 roadmap, not AMD and Intel RTX Spark agentic AI platform fuels a hunger for storage bandwidth
summary: Nvidia may lead in PCIe Gen6 client PC platforms, influencing suppliers like Silicon Motion.
insight: Nvidia's AI ambitions drive its PCIe 6.0 roadmap ahead of competitors

title: AMD silently removes memory encryption from consumer Ryzen CPUs, leaving users unaware that they may be vulnerable security feature vanishes after newer AGESA firmware, AMD engineers go radio silent when pressed about the change
summary: AMD has reportedly removed TSME, a memory-encryption feature, from consumer Ryzen CPUs. Newer AGESA firmware disables the feature, leaving users vulnerable. Pro and EPYC CPUs remain unaffected.
insight: Security feature removal compromises sensitive data, risking user confidentiality and integrity.

title: Chinese memory brands ditch Samsung and Micron for homegrown CXMT and YMTC silicon Corsair, HP, and Dell are already adopting the China-produced DDR5 chips
summary: Chinese memory brands Gloway and KingBank are adopting homegrown silicon from CXMT and YMTC to produce DDR5 memory kits, replacing foreign DRAM suppliers like Samsung, Micron, and SK hynix.
insight: China's memory industry is rapidly reducing reliance on foreign suppliers.

title: Intel s performance-enhanced 18A-P process enters risk production drop-in 18A upgrade promises 9% performance improvement at iso-power, cuts thermal resistance by 40%
summary: Intel's enhanced 18A-P process has entered risk production, setting stage for full production ramp-up. This process promises a 9% performance improvement at iso-power and reduces thermal resistance by 40%. The upgrade aims to enhance overall system performance and efficiency.
insight: Enhanced performance and reduced thermal resistance boost system efficiency.

title: Dell's $699 XPS 13 laptop is now available with the MacBook Neo in its sights entry-level XPS design comes with Wildcat Lake, 8GB of RAM, and a 512GB SSD
summary: Dell's $699 XPS 13 now available with Wildcat Lake CPU, 8GB RAM, and 512GB SSD.
insight: Dell's budget laptop challenges Apple's MacBook Neo market dominance

title: SanDisk Optimus GX Pro 8100 8TB SSD Review the undisputed king of high-capacity PCIe 5.0 SSDs
summary: The SanDisk Optimus GX Pro 8100 excels in performance and efficiency. With its high-capacity PCIe 5.0 capabilities, it meets the demands of data-intensive workloads. However, its price might be a limiting factor.
insight: Unmatched performance for high-capacity data-intensive workloads requires significant investment

title: Logitech's new G305 X Superlight weighs just 59 grams and costs $79 company also releases hot-swappable G316 X 98 keyboard with pixel display, translucent control knob
summary: Logitech releases the G305 X Superlight mouse and G316 X 98 keyboard, both designed for gaming performance and customization.
insight: Logitech enhances gaming peripherals for competitive players with high-performance features.

title: 'The retail SSD market has almost disappeared,' says Silicon Motion exec PC OEMs are buying third-party drives as direct NAND supply dries up
summary: The retail SSD market is dwindling as NAND suppliers focus on AI data centers. PC OEMs are now purchasing third-party SSDs due to the direct NAND supply shortage. This shift is impacting the availability of retail SSDs.
insight: NAND manufacturers prioritize AI data centers over retail SSD demand

title: Chinese fab SMIC's 7nm metal pitch beats Intel 18A but lags 38% on density, teardown finds Huawei's sanctions-beating HiSilicon Kirin 9030 is the first subject of SemiAnalysis's new teardown lab
summary: SMIC's 7nm metal pitch is 32.5nm, beating Intel's 18A but lagging in density.
insight: Advancements in Chinese fab technology narrow global semiconductor gap.

title: Noctua finally releases its first AIO coolers prices start at $220 for 240mm, features the company s legendary A-series fans
summary: Noctua releases its NL-LC1 AIO coolers with prices starting at $219.95. The 240mm option includes an optional 80mm auxiliary fan. This aims to enhance cooling performance in PCs.
insight: Noctua combines legendary fans with AIO coolers for optimal performance.

title: 18 funky tech gift ideas for Father's Day 2026 get something a little different for dad this year
summary: Celebrate Father's Day by getting your dad a unique tech gift, showcasing your creativity and thoughtfulness. Consider his interests and hobbies when selecting a gift. This will make the gift even more special and meaningful.
insight: Personalized tech gifts show dad you care about his passions.

title: SMI's PCIe 6.0 SSD controller for consumer SSDs coming next year, but severe NAND shortages will get even worse in 2027 as AI data centers swallow supply An interview with Silicon Motion's SVP Nelson Duann
summary: Silicon Motion's SVP Nelson Duann discusses the NAND supply crisis and future consumer storage trends.
insight: NAND shortages worsen due to AI data centers' expanding demand.

title: Zhihe A210 octa-core RISC-V SoC with 12 TOPS NPU powers SoM-based development board
summary: The Zhihe A210 is an octa-core RISC-V SoC with a 12 TOPS NPU, featuring 4x 64-bit RISC-V C920 cores at up to 2.3 GHz. This SoC powers a SoM-based development board, offering improved performance and AI capabilities. The A210 SODIMM V2 development kit is available for testing and development.
insight: High-performance RISC-V SoC boosts AI capabilities and processing power.

title: Amlogic A311Y3 octa-core Edge AI SoC features Cortex-A78/A55 cores, 8 TOPS NPU, LPDDR5 support
summary: Amlogic introduces the A311Y3 Edge AI SoC with 6nm Cortex-A78/A55 cores and 8 TOPS NPU.
insight: Amlogic A311Y3 enhances edge AI capabilities with improved CPU and NPU performance.

title: SCINTIX P4 ESP32-P4 Compute Module works with (some) Raspberry Pi CM4/CM5 carrier boards (Crowdfunding)
summary: The SCINTIX P4 is an ESP32-P4 Compute Module compatible with some Raspberry Pi CM4/CM5 carrier boards, offering wireless connectivity and access to various peripherals. It features a dual-core RISC-V CPU and supports programming in standalone mode. The module is currently available through crowdfunding.
insight: First MCU-based system-on-module for Raspberry Pi CM4/CM5 form factor

title: Snap says its $2,195 Specs are AR glasses for the post-smartphone era
summary: Snap introduces Specs, AR glasses for the post-smartphone era, available for pre-order with a $200 refundable deposit. Specs are expected to ship this fall in the US, UK, and France. This marks a significant step in the evolution of augmented reality.
insight: Marking a shift from smartphones to immersive, wearable technology experiences.

title: AMD quietly disabled RAM encryption on some consumer Ryzen CPUs and users want to know why
summary: AMD disabled RAM encryption on some Ryzen CPUs without user notification, sparking concerns about data security.
insight: Users rely on hardware security features for sensitive data protection.

title: Sony patents PlayStation controller buttons that get harder, softer, and grab your fingers as you play
summary: Sony has patented a PlayStation controller with adaptive buttons that change hardness and grip as you play, potentially enhancing player experience and interaction.
insight: Innovative haptic feedback technology redefines gaming controller interaction.

title: AMD Zen 6 desktop CPUs may ditch integrated graphics for a built-in NPU
summary: AMD's Zen 6 'Morpheus' processors may abandon integrated graphics for a built-in NPU, marking a significant shift in the architecture.
insight: Integrated graphics replacement with NPU enhances AI capabilities significantly.

title: The "Made in America" Trump phone is just a reskinned HTC U24 built in China
summary: A teardown of the Trump Mobile T1 revealed it's an HTC U24 Pro with minor cosmetic changes. The phone's manufacturing origins contradict Trump Mobile's original claim of being American-made. This settles suspicions raised earlier this year.
insight: False advertising undermines American manufacturing claims with a Trump-branded phone.

title: Microsoft Teams is getting Wi-Fi location "check-in," but it's less creepy than it sounds
summary: Microsoft Teams is introducing a Wi-Fi location 'check-in' feature, allowing users to control their location settings. The feature will provide employees' physical locations to their organizations. Microsoft has clarified how this feature will work and be managed.
insight: Location tracking in Teams is an extension, not a new layer of surveillance.

title: Noctua launches its first AIO liquid CPU coolers with a range of radiator sizes
summary: Noctua's new AIO liquid CPU coolers feature a three-layer acoustic soundproofing structure and a pump speed toggle for improved performance and quiet operation.
insight: Innovative soundproofing technology enhances cooling performance and reduces noise.

title: Commodore's Callback 8020 is a $499 flip phone that blocks social media and browsers
summary: The Commodore Callback 8020 is a $499 flip phone that blocks social media and browsers, positioning itself between a basic feature phone and a full Android device.
insight: Reviving the 'dumb phone' concept with a modern, high-end twist

title: Firefox adds new tab tools, JPEG XL, cleaner Settings page
summary: Firefox 152 brings new tab tools, a refreshed Settings page, and JPEG XL support.
insight: Improved browser usability and performance with enhanced features.

title: Microsoft is making Windows 11 updates require just one reboot instead of several
summary: Microsoft is updating Windows 11 to require a single reboot for all updates.
insight: Smaller system downtime improves user productivity and efficiency significantly.

title: SpaceX acquires AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion, days after record IPO
summary: SpaceX acquires Cursor for $60 billion, capitalizing on its post-IPO stock surge.
insight: SpaceX leverages inflated stock value to seal strategic AI acquisition.

title: Logitech completes G3 lineup with budget gaming keyboard and mouse
summary: Logitech introduces the G305 X Superlight, a budget gaming mouse with improved features. Priced at $79.99, it offers an updated sensor, scroll wheel, and RGB lighting. The device supports up to five on-board profiles for customization.
insight: Mid-range gaming peripherals offer affordable high-performance options for gamers.

title: UK confirms social media ban for kids is coming Meta, YouTube, and Snapchat respond
summary: The UK confirms plans to ban social media for users under 16, following the lead of other countries like Australia.
insight: Protecting children's mental health and well-being is a top priority worldwide.
